analysis.name: Segment
analysis.tagline: Collect real-time data in unified profiles—and so much more
analysis.value_proposition: Collect, clean, and activate first-party customer data across 750+ integrations to create unified profiles and orchestrate personalized experiences at scale.
analysis.ideal_customer: Engineering, data, and marketing teams at growth-stage to enterprise companies needing a single hub to collect, govern, and activate customer data across many downstream tools

features.name: connections
features.description: Customer data pipeline to collect first-party data from every touchpoint and route to 550+ destinations
features.evidence_quote: Collect first-party data from every touchpoint and activate it to the data warehouse or one of 550+ destinations

features.name: sources
features.description: SDKs and libraries to ingest data from web, mobile, server, and cloud apps
features.evidence_quote: Capture data from any source, enforce quality with schemas, unify customer identity

features.name: destinations
features.description: Send customer event data to 550+ third-party tools and platforms without re-instrumentation
features.evidence_quote: activate it to the data warehouse or one of 550+ destinations for a unified customer view

features.name: unify
features.description: Identity resolution that stitches cross-device and cross-channel events into a single persistent customer profile
features.evidence_quote: Create unified profiles, build advanced audiences, orchestrate real-time journeys

features.name: engage
features.description: Audience segmentation and multi-channel journey orchestration product built on unified profiles
features.evidence_quote: Create unified profiles, build advanced audiences, orchestrate real-time journeys, and enhance any experience with data from the warehouse

features.name: audiences
features.description: Build, preview, and activate behavioral and profile-based audience segments

features.name: journeys
features.description: Visual multi-step journey builder to orchestrate real-time cross-channel customer experiences

features.name: protocols
features.description: Data governance layer with tracking plans to enforce schema quality and catch bad events
features.evidence_quote: Protocols Overview, Create a Tracking Plan, Data Collection Best Practices

features.name: tracking plan
features.description: Schema definition tool to standardize event names, properties, and required fields across teams
features.evidence_quote: Create a Tracking Plan, Tracking Plan Libraries, Validate With Violations

features.name: tracking plan libraries
features.description: Reusable, shareable event schema libraries to propagate standards across workspaces

features.name: schema controls
features.description: Block or allow events based on whether they conform to the defined schema
features.evidence_quote: Enforce With Data Controls, Customize Your Schema Controls

features.name: transform
features.description: In-flight transformation to fix malformed or non-conforming event data before delivery

features.name: forward violations
features.description: Route schema-violating events to a separate destination for inspection instead of dropping them

features.name: anomaly detection
features.description: Automated detection of unexpected changes in event volume or schema structure

features.name: typewriter
features.description: Type-safe analytics client generator that auto-generates strongly-typed code from tracking plans

features.name: warehouses
features.description: Sync raw customer event and object data to cloud data warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, etc.)

features.name: reverse etl
features.description: Sync models and audiences from the data warehouse back into downstream activation tools

features.name: privacy portal
features.description: Centralized UI for managing data subject requests, PII suppression, and privacy compliance
features.evidence_quote: Privacy Portal, Detect PII, Data Controls and Alerts

features.name: pii detection
features.description: Automatically detect and optionally block PII from flowing into downstream destinations

features.name: consent management
features.description: Collect, store, and enforce user consent preferences across connections, audiences, and reverse ETL
features.evidence_quote: Consent Management Overview, Consent in Segment Connections, Configure Consent Management

features.name: gdpr compliance
features.description: Built-in tooling for GDPR including data deletion workflows and user suppression

features.name: hipaa eligibility
features.description: HIPAA-eligible infrastructure configuration for healthcare and regulated data handling

features.name: user deletion and suppression
features.description: Delete or permanently suppress specific user's data across all connected destinations
features.evidence_quote: User Deletion and Suppression, Account & Data Deletion

features.name: data retention policy
features.description: Configurable data retention windows with automated deletion after expiry

features.name: sso
features.description: SAML-based single sign-on for enterprise workspace authentication

features.name: scim
features.description: Automated user provisioning and deprovisioning via the SCIM protocol
features.evidence_quote: System for Cross-domain Identity Management (SCIM) Configuration Guide

features.name: mfa
features.description: Multi-factor authentication enforcement for workspace user accounts

features.name: audit trail
features.description: Immutable log of all workspace configuration changes and user actions

features.name: label-based access control
features.description: Fine-grained RBAC using labels to restrict team access to specific sources and destinations

features.name: roles and permissions
features.description: Role-based access control with predefined and custom roles for workspace members
features.evidence_quote: Identity & Access Management Overview, Concepts, Roles, Manage Workspace Access

features.name: monitor and alerts
features.description: Real-time pipeline health monitoring with default system alerts and configurable custom alerts
features.evidence_quote: Monitor Overview, Alerts Overview, Default Alerts, Custom Alerts

features.name: failure log collection
features.description: Capture and inspect events that failed delivery to destinations for debugging

features.name: replay
features.description: Re-send historical event data to a new or existing destination without re-instrumentation

features.name: regional data residency
features.description: Store and process customer data in specific geographic regions to satisfy compliance requirements

features.name: dbt extension
features.description: Native dbt integration to sync dbt models and expose warehouse transformations in Segment

features.name: git extension
features.description: Version-control tracking plans and workspace configurations via Git

features.name: public api
features.description: REST API for programmatic management of sources, destinations, and workspace resources

features.name: config api
features.description: API for programmatic configuration of all Segment workspace resources
features.evidence_quote: Config API overview, API design, Authentication

features.name: segment query language
features.description: SQL-like query language for filtering and querying event data within Segment

features.name: destination filter query language
features.description: Expression language for conditionally filtering individual events before sending to a destination

features.name: event filtering
features.description: Control which events and properties flow to each destination via filter rules

features.name: production and testing environments
features.description: Separate production and staging environments within a single Segment workspace
features.evidence_quote: Prod and Testing Environments in Segment

features.name: javascript sdk (analytics.js)
features.description: Browser-side analytics SDK for web event collection with auto-page tracking and bundled integrations

features.name: mobile sdks
features.description: Native SDKs for iOS (Swift) and Android (Kotlin) mobile event collection

features.name: server-side sdks
features.description: Server-side libraries for C#, Java, and other backend runtimes
features.evidence_quote: C#, Java, Javascript, Kotlin (Android), Swift, Other sources

features.name: integrations catalog
features.description: Browsable catalog of 750+ pre-built source and destination integrations
features.evidence_quote: Power the tools you already use with your data by enabling any of our 750+ integrations.

features.name: mtu-based billing
features.description: Usage-based billing model metered on Monthly Tracked Users (MTUs) and throughput volume

features.name: real-time data pipeline
features.description: Stream customer events in real time from collection through to destination delivery
features.evidence_quote: Collect real-time data in unified profiles

features.name: first-party data collection
features.description: Instrument owned properties to capture first-party behavioral and identity data
features.evidence_quote: Collect first-party data from every touchpoint

features.name: cross-channel identity resolution
features.description: Track and merge customer identity across web, mobile, email, and offline channels
features.evidence_quote: Tracking Customers Across Channels and Devices

features.name: historical data import
features.description: Backfill historical customer event data from legacy systems into Segment

features.name: duplicate data handling
features.description: Detect and suppress duplicate events to prevent double-counting in downstream tools

features.name: bot filtering
features.description: Identify and filter internet bot traffic from customer event streams

features.name: multi-channel re-engagement
features.description: Automated multi-channel re-engagement campaign orchestration for lapsed users

features.name: push notification support
features.description: Create and trigger mobile push notifications based on Segment events and audiences

features.name: event-triggered notifications
features.description: Set up alerts and operational notifications triggered by specific customer events
features.evidence_quote: Setting Up Event-Triggered Notifications or Alerts

features.name: ad spend optimization
features.description: Activate first-party audiences to advertising platforms to optimize ad targeting

features.name: attribution support
features.description: Capture and route event data to support marketing attribution and ROI measurement
features.evidence_quote: Measuring the ROI of Your Marketing Campaigns, Segment's Role in Attribution

features.name: ltv forecasting
features.description: SQL-based customer lifetime value forecasting guide for e-commerce
features.evidence_quote: Forecasting LTV with SQL and Excel for E-Commerce

features.name: dynamic coupon program
features.description: Build event-driven loyalty reward and coupon workflows using customer profile data
features.evidence_quote: Setting up a Dynamic Coupon Program to Reward Loyal Customers

features.name: support access
features.description: Temporarily grant Segment support staff scoped access to a workspace for troubleshooting

features.name: workspace home
features.description: Central dashboard showing pipeline health, recent activity, and workspace overview

features.name: data controls and alerts
features.description: Configure policy-based alerts when data quality issues or governance violations are detected

features.name: ai assistant (help center)
features.description: AI-powered chat assistant for answering questions and troubleshooting in the help center
features.evidence_quote: Chat with our AI assistant to get answers to your questions, troubleshoot, or find helpful information.

differentiators: 750+ pre-built integrations — largest catalog in the CDP category
Part of Twilio platform — native communications (SMS, WhatsApp, RCS, voice, email) activation alongside CDP without additional vendors
Collect once, route everywhere — single instrumentation eliminates per-tool tracking code
Real-time event streaming with sub-second latency to 550+ destinations
HIPAA-eligible infrastructure for regulated healthcare use cases
Regional data residency for compliance-sensitive global customers
Developer-first architecture with typed SDKs, public API, and Typewriter code generation
End-to-end platform: collect → govern (Protocols) → unify (Unify) → activate (Engage) → communicate (Twilio) on one stack
Gartner Magic Quadrant Leader for CPaaS (2026) — highest Ability to Execute

praised: 'Track once, send everywhere' architecture dramatically reduces engineering instrumentation burden
Large integrations catalog (750+) is a primary reason teams choose Segment
Developer experience: typed SDKs, strong docs, and open-source libraries on GitHub
Monolith architecture decision discussed positively: 'Why Segment Went Back to a Monolith' (604 upvotes, 316 comments on HN)
complaints: G2 review data was not available in provided sources
MTU-based pricing model can become expensive at scale for high-traffic products (inferred from docs billing section prominence)

free_tier.limits: Limits not specified in scraped pricing page content; 'Start for free' CTA present on landing

pricing.notes: Pricing page content did not render tier cards in the scraped source — only nav and header loaded. Billing is MTU (Monthly Tracked Users) + throughput based per docs ('MTUs, Throughput and Billing'). 'Start for free' CTA on landing confirms free tier exists. Full tier pricing structure not available in provided source data. Changelog returned 404.

changelog.text:
What's new in Google Analytics - Analytics Help
Skip to main content
Analytics Help
Sign in
Send feedback on...
This help content & information
General Help Center experience
Next
Help Center
Community
Announcements
Analytics
Contact us
Get started with Analytics
Collect and manage data

Advertising and attribution
Audiences and remarketing
Manage accounts, properties, and users
Google Analytics 360
Policies and data privacy
What's new in Google Analytics
This article provides information on the latest releases in Google Analytics for this year. For information on releases from past years, see the
What's New archive
.
Releases
June 11, 2026
June 8, 2026
May 13, 2026
May 7, 2026
May 4, 2026
April 29, 2026
February 10, 2026
January 16, 2026
June 11, 2026
New Source Group field with update to Source Platform values for improved source analysis
Source grouping is a new dimension concept that consolidates source values for common online platforms - Facebook, Instagram, and TikTok. A single value for a given source is vital for performance and attribution analysis (example: “Facebook”; not “facebook”, “fb”, and “Meta-facebook”), and the Source Group field consolidates various source values to provide that single reporting value.
Source Platform is an existing field that we are updating and improving some classifications to align with our updates for Source Group.
This update enables advertisers to:
Better analyze and optimize their cross-channel performance
- Source grouping helps to standardize source dimension values in order to improve your budget analysis and source reporting. You will be able to identify performance and optimization opportunities from both first and third-party platforms and inventory sources. This makes cross-channel reporting in the advertising section more simple and actionable.
Standardize reporting
- This update provides the same level of granularity for third-party platforms (TikTok, Pinterest, Amazon) as it does for Google inventory (YouTube, Search, Maps) and automatically bundles disparate messy source values (example: %instagram%, ig) into clean, high-level categories like Instagram (Source Group) /Meta Ads (Source Platform).
Future-proof for AI
- Includes built-in grouping for emerging traffic sources like ChatGPT (OpenAI) and Perplexity.
Review retroactive insights
- As Source Group is populated retroactively, you can use dimensions to analyze your historical data to take action and optimize advertising.
Hostname filter
Hostname filters are a new type of filter within the existing Data Filter section of the Admin section. It allows customers to filter out (exclude) events based on their hostname (example: “
google.com
”).
This enables you to make sure any data that does not come from an approved domain is not collected, and therefore can’t distract from your desired analysis.
June 8, 2026
New Google Business Profile integration
You can now centrally report on your local business, website, and app metrics. Link your Google Analytics properties with Google Business Profiles (GBP) directly within the Admin panel. This integration enables the seamless import of high-value first-party GBP data. A new dedicated reporting collection surfaces key GBP metrics including interactions, phone calls, bookings, direction requests, and website clicks.
Metric details:
Access seven core GBP metrics directly in your reports: GBP interactions, calls, bookings, directions, website clicks, messages, and menus.
Reporting
: A new dedicated collection for Google Business Profiles is automatically added to the Reports menu upon linking, providing a structured view of how your local assets drive engagement.
Data availability
: GBP metrics are available on a rolling 6-month window. If a selected date range extends beyond this period, Analytics will load the most recent data available from the profile.
This integration helps bridge the gap for measuring in-store intent by providing proxy metrics for physical visits. By centralizing these insights, businesses can better understand the full customer journey and how assets like Search and Maps contribute to overall cross-channel value.
May 13, 2026
New AI Assistant traffic measurement
Google Analytics now provides a dedicated way to measure and analyze traffic originating from popular AI assistants. You can now identify how users are discovering your site through chatbots like ChatGPT, Gemini, and Claude via a new
AI Assistant
channel in your Default Channel Group reports.
This update introduces the following changes to your traffic source dimensions:
Medium
: A new "ai-assistant" value is automatically assigned when the referrer matches a recognized AI Assistant
Channel Group
: These visits are categorized under the "AI Assistant" channel
Campaign
: Traffic from these sources will be identified with the "(ai-assistant)" campaign name
This feature helps you monitor how generative AI impacts your business by tracking user clicks, trending AI sources, and how this traffic compares to traditional channels like organic search.
May 7, 2026
Data Manager API support for Google Analytics server-to-server events
Data Manager API
provides you with an alternative to
Measurement Protocol
(MP) for sending recommended and custom events directly to your Google Analytics property. You can send events to both your Google Analytics web and app data streams, provided they’re not reserved events.
Learn more about the steps to upgrade
May 4, 2026
Conversion support in the Data API
This feature may not be available to your Google Analytics property. The Google Analytics team is actively working to expand this feature to more properties. Please reach out to your support team if you have questions about the eligibility of your property.
Cross-channel
conversion reporting data
is now available from the Google Analytics Data API in alpha for Google Analytics and Google Ads customers. Google Analytics developers can now gain programmatic access to the same paid and organic reporting data that's available in the Analytics advertising section's Conversion performance report. Refer to the
Google Analytics Data API changelog
for more details about this new API surface.
April 29, 2026
Configure your Google Analytics settings using Task Assistant
To help you get the most out of your Google Analytics property, we are introducing Task Assistant. Task Assistant provides tailored recommendations to help you optimize your configuration and improve data collection,. Available from the left navigation menu, it organizes these recommendations into actionable categories, such as connecting your accounts, enhancing your reporting, and fixing data issues. You can easily manage your property's progress by marking tasks as complete as you finish them, or skipping tasks that aren't relevant to your current business goals. Return back to the Task Assistant for additional tasks.
Learn more about Task Assistant
February 10, 2026
Generated insights on the Home page
We’ve added
Generated insights
to your Home page. This feature summarizes the top three data changes since your last visit, including key configuration updates, anomalies, and seasonality trends.This helps you quickly catch up on what’s happening in your property and spot performance shifts without needing to dig into detailed reports.
Learn more about Generated insights
Cross-channel budgeting (Beta)
This feature may not be available to your Google Analytics property. The Google Analytics team is actively working to expand this feature to more properties. Please reach out to your support team if you have questions about the eligibility of your property.
The Google Analytics team is excited to announce the release of cross-channel budgeting. Use cross-channel budgeting to track performance and optimize paid channel investments.
January 16, 2026
Cross-channel budgeting (Beta)
This feature may not be available to your Google Analytics property. The Google Analytics team is actively working to expand this feature to more properties. Please reach out to your support team if you have questions about the eligibility of your property.
The Google Analytics team is excited to announce the release of cross-channel budgeting. Use cross-channel budgeting to track performance and optimize paid channel investments.
Projection plans
help you identify areas for improvement and make quick optimization decisions. You can see how advertising channels are expected to perform against your performance indicators (KPIs) like spend, conversions, and revenue.
Scenario plans
help you determine the optimal distribution of your budget for future media initiatives by letting you explore potential ROI at different budget levels.
With these tools, you'll be able to answer critical questions like, "Is my spend on track?", "How many future conversions are forecasted based on my planned spend?" and “How should I allocate my budgets to maximize revenue and ROI?”
Learn more about cross-channel budgeting
Improved web conversion management and reporting capabilities for Google Ads customers (Beta)
This feature may not be available to your Google Analytics property. The Google Analytics team is actively working to expand this feature to more properties. Please reach out to your support team if you have questions about the eligibility of your property.
More flexible web conversion management tools and new cross-channel reporting features are now available for marketers and advertisers.
Conversion attribution settings are now adjustable independently for every conversion, enabling customers to fine-tune their bidding strategies in Google Ads, eliminate a common cause of conversion reporting discrepancies versus Google Ads, and gain greater confidence in the efficacy of their ad spend.
We’re also adding more comprehensive cross-channel reporting features and capabilities, including new reporting dimension filters, to unlock new insights.
Learn more about cross-channel conversion reporting in Analytics
Conversion attribution analysis report (Beta)
This feature may not be available to your Google Analytics property. The Google Analytics team is actively working to expand this feature to more properties. Please reach out to your support team if you have questions about the eligibility of your property.
The
Conversion attribution analysis
report is a new addition to the advertising workspace that provides customers with the ability to uncover the full value of their marketing channels across the entire customer journey.
This report helps you visualize how different touchpoints contribute to your bottom line through two specialized views:
Assisted conversions (Last Click)
:
This view allows you to see "Assists"—touchpoints that engaged customers early in their journey but weren't the final click. This is essential for identifying undervalued upper-funnel channels, such as YouTube or Demand Gen, that drive interest before a final search or direct visit.
Refined funnel analysis (Data-driven attribution)
:
This view categorizes touchpoints into Early, Mid, and Late stages. We now separate single-touchpoint paths from multi-touchpoint journeys, giving you a clearer picture of which campaigns are closing complex journeys versus those that act as standalone drivers.
By using these new insights, advertisers can better justify upper-funnel spend and build a more holistic budget strategy that rewards every stage of the funnel.

integrations.title: Analytics & Data Analysis Features List - Analytics
integrations.text:
Analytics & Data Analysis Features List - Analytics
Marketing Platform
For Small Businesses
For Enterprises
Resources
Blog
Partners
Support
Analytics
Overview
Benefits
Features
Sign in to Analytics
Get started today
For Small Businesses
Free and easy-to-use solutions for businesses of every size.
Learn more
For Enterprises
A single platform that integrates your advertising and analytics, so you can act on customer insights faster.
Learn more
Analytics
Understand your customers so you can deliver better experiences.
Tag Manager
Manage all your tags without editing code.
advertising
Campaign Manager 360
Get a complete view of all your digital media campaigns.
Display & Video 360
Reach today’s always-connected audiences wherever they are.
Search Ads 360

analytics
Analytics 360
Use advanced tools to get a deeper understanding of your customers so you can deliver better experiences.
Tag Manager 360
Manage all your tags in one place for a smarter, simpler way to oversee your marketing.
Marketing Platform
Analytics
Overview
Benefits
Features
Sign in to Analytics
Get started today
The finer points.
Analytics helps you understand how people use your sites and apps, so you can take action to improve their experience. Discover what Google Analytics can do by checking out the features listed below.
Built-in Automation
Get fast answers to questions about your Analytics data, predict user behavior, and tap into powerful modeling capabilities.
Expand all
Collapse all
Predictive Capabilities
By applying Google’s machine learning models, Analytics can analyze your data and predict future actions people may take, like making a purchase or churning. You can then create audiences that are predicted to take these actions to drive conversions or retain more of your users.
Proactive Insights
Analytics will automatically detect and surface actionable insights from your data like important changes, new trends, and other growth opportunities you should be aware of.
Privacy-Safe Modeling
Google's machine learning models provide a more complete picture of the customer journey, enhancing your understanding of customer behavior and conversions based on your observed, first-party, and consented data.
Answers to Your Questions
Ask questions in natural language using the search bar to quickly find the metric, report, or insight you’re looking for.
Reporting
See at a glance how customers interact with your sites and apps, and easily navigate to reports to dive deeper.
Expand all
Collapse all
Real-Time Reporting
Monitor activity on your site or app as it happens.
Acquisition Reports
See how users land on your site or app and understand the effectiveness of your marketing.
User Acquisition
Discover how users reach your site or app through different paid and organic sources.
Traffic Acquisition
See a session-based view of traffic and engagement on your site or app through different paid and organic traffic sources.
Engagement Reports
Better understand what content drives engagement and conversions on your site or app.
Events Report
Get a detailed view of user actions, system events, or errors.
Conversion Report
See how all your marketing channels are working together to drive conversions.
Pages and Screen Report
See which web pages and app screens users engage with the most.
Monetization Reports
See how much revenue your site or app generates whether it’s from ecommerce, subscriptions, or ads.
Ecommerce
Analyze purchase activity including product and transaction information, average purchase revenue, average purchase revenue per user, and other data.
In-App Purchases
Improve your app monetization with insights about the highest performing products and subscriptions.
Publisher Ads
See ad revenue that your app generates using the
Google Analytics for Firebase SDK
.
Data Freshness (Analytics 360 Only)
Get continuous intraday data via Analytics 360’s interface and the API. Data usually appears within an hour after collection, so you can make faster decisions.
Sub-Properties (Analytics 360 Only)
Create separate views of your data for different countries or product lines.
Roll-Up Reporting (Analytics 360 Only)
Get a combined view of all data from your Analytics 360 properties to see global metrics, or create separate views of data across countries or product lines.
Advertising Workspace
Better understand the ROI of your media spend across all channels, make informed decisions about budget allocation, and evaluate attribution models.
Expand all
Collapse all
Advertising Snapshot
Quickly address everyday advertising needs and unlock deeper insights into your campaign performance from Google Ads, Display & Video 360, and Search Ads 360.
Attribution
Understand the value of your marketing with cross-platform attribution reporting to evaluate each touchpoint in your marketing funnel.
Data-Driven Attribution
Use Google’s machine learning to understand the contribution of each touchpoint in your marketing funnel.
Conversion Paths Report
Understand your customers’ paths to conversion, and how different attribution models distribute credit on those paths.
Model Comparison Report
Compare how different attribution models impact the valuation of your marketing channels.
Explorations
Access, organize, and visualize your data to fit your business needs. Run deeper, custom analysis of your data without the limitations of pre-defined reports, and share insights with other users.
Expand all
Collapse all
Free-Form Exploration
Conduct ad-hoc analysis by dragging and dropping multiple variables — the different segments, dimensions, and metrics you use to measure your business — onto a canvas to see instant visualizations of your data.
Funnel Exploration
Visualize the steps users take to complete tasks on your site or app, and see how you can optimize user experience and identify over- or under-performing audiences — with both open and closed funnel options.
Segment Overlap
See how different user segments relate to each other. Use this technique to identify new segments of users who meet complex criteria.
Path Exploration
Visualize your users’ paths to conversion as they interact with your website and app.
Cohort Exploration
Gain insight into the behavior and performance changes of users grouped by common attributes.
User Exploration
Select specific groups of users and drill down deeper to understand how those users engage with your site or app.
User Lifetime
Explore user behavior and value over their lifetime as a customer.
Backwards Pathing
Work backwards from a conversion or other key event and analyze the differences, trends, or patterns in the paths users took to get there.
Unsampled Explorations
Create unsampled explorations to access raw data, run more advanced analysis, and discover more granular insights. And in Analytics 360, run unsampled explorations for up to 50 billion events per day.
Data Collection and Management
Collect, organize, and manage your data easily with a variety of integrated tools.
Expand all
Collapse all
Collection APIs
Analytics supports Javascript libraries, mobile app SDKs, and an open measurement protocol that allow you to import interaction data from any internet-connected third-party system.
Tag Management Support
Analytics is supported by all leading tag management systems and works seamlessly with
Google Tag Manager
and
Tag Manager 360
.
Admin API
Manage your Analytics configuration programmatically with our
admin API.
Custom Definitions
Import customized data easily. Custom definitions, including custom dimensions and metrics, can measure a variety of structured or unstructured text or measurements.
Data Import
Securely import your first-party data and use it with your Analytics data for a more complete view of the customer journey.
Google BigQuery Export
An integration with Google BigQuery allows you to seamlessly export your Analytics data to
BigQuery
where you can conduct interactive analysis of up to trillions of rows of data, join multiple data sources, do advanced predictive modeling, and more.
Enhanced Limits (Analytics 360 Only)
Analytics 360 has higher limits for up to 125 custom dimensions, 400 audiences, and 50 conversion types.
Automatic Custom Tables (Analytics 360 Only)
Get fast, high cardinality tables for your most important data sets automatically with automatic custom tables.
Integrations
Analytics is designed to work seamlessly with other Google solutions and partner products, saving you time and increasing efficiency.
Expand all
Collapse all
Google Ads
See your
Ads
data together with your website and app performance data in the Google Ads reports in Analytics. You can also import your Analytics conversions and audiences into your Google Ads account.
Display & Video 360
See and analyze your
Display & Video 360
data in Analytics. Export conversions created in Analytics and use them for automated bidding in Display & Video 360. Create audiences in Analytics and use them in Display & Video 360.
Search Ads 360
See and analyze your
Search Ads 360
data in Analytics. Export conversions created in Analytics and use them for automated bidding in Search Ads 360. Create audiences in Analytics and use them in Search Ads 360.
Google Cloud
With a built-in connection to
BigQuery
, Google Cloud’s enterprise data warehouse, you can easily join Analytics data with other datasets and unlock BigQuery’s powerful tools for identifying insights.
Salesforce Marketing Cloud Integration
Data from Analytics is visible in the
Marketing Cloud
reporting UI for a more complete understanding of campaign performance. Your Marketing Cloud emails will be automatically tagged with UTM parameters so that you can use Analytics to understand how effective your emails are.
Google Ad Manager
Apply your Analytics insights to
Google Ad Manager
to optimize the user experience on your sites and apps, and improve monetization.
Google AdMob
Measure your AdMob app revenue.
Google Play
Track
Google Play
purchase and subscription revenue for your app.
Google Search Console
See Search Console data in Analytics and get insight into which Search queries drove users to your site, and how your organic Search performance translates to on-site action.

tiers.name: Google Analytics (Standard)

pricing.notes: Public pricing page does not disclose Analytics 360 rates; historically ~$150K/yr floor via sales. Standard GA4 is free but subject to event volume caps and sampling on very large properties.

New
Saved Views
Ditch complex, intrusive web analytics for Fathom - a better Google Analytics alternative. Experience ease of use, forever data retention, and complete, worry-free GDPR compliance - all while protecting
your time
and your visitors'
digital privacy
.
Start free trial
View a live demo
Quick setup
Free 7-day trial
No obligation
Analytics for Huberman Lab are solely powered by Fathom. It's such a pleasure to use compared to Google Analytics. There's just the right number of features, and their platform is incredibly intuitive.
Ian Mackey, Huberman Lab
Trusted by IBM, GitHub, Tailwind & over a million websites
Ditch Google Analytics
At Fathom, we strongly believe that analytics tools should be insightful, not invasive. This is why we created a privacy-focused, simple software tool that offers valuable insights without the complexity.
Get setup in minutes
Our script is
a single line of code
that works with any website, CMS or framework. Set up in minutes and start collecting real-time data without prior technical knowledge. Our
analytics software
is designed to be easily understood instantly.
Comply with privacy laws
We've hired the best lawyers and legal minds worldwide to ensure our simple analytics software is
fully compliant
with GDPR, CCPA, ePrivacy, PECR and more, providing essential insights into traffic sources without compromising privacy.
See more accurate data
Our real-time analytics blocks bots, scrapers and spam traffic—showing you only real, human visits. This gives you the flexibility to collect data for (and only pay for) exactly the data you want to see and make decisions about.
Say no to cookie banners
Fathom
anonymizes IP addresses
and other visitor data
without using cookies
. That means you don't have to clutter your site or slow it down with cookie consent banners and notices for your site's analytics.
Join the movement
Thousands of companies, including Fortune 100s, pioneers of the Open Web, and governments, have
switched from Google Analytics
to Fathom for ease of use, better privacy, and more accurate metrics.
Different. In a good way.
For a long time, web analytics tools have been seriously broken. Pages upon pages of reports to click. Training and certifications required. And, worst of all, it was offered for “free,” so tech companies could profit wildly from all of our personal data.
Our software is something quite different. Our software protects your time and your visitors' digital privacy.
Wow, what a
breath of fresh air
. I love how simple Fathom is, and it really gives you all the traffic metrics you could need.
Joel Gascoigne
CEO of Buffer
I switched to Fathom because it’s an ethical analytics tool
that I trust
. It gives me everything I want to know about my site's performance.
Charli Marie
Creative director of ConvertKit
I consider Fathom a
must have
tool if you want to understand what's happening with your website traffic—it's simple, speedy, and accurate.
Ruben Gamez
Founder Signwell & Bidsketch
After switching to Fathom for my web properties, I find I'm more
attentive
to my sites because I'm always aware of how many people are on them and which pages are most popular.
Caleb Porzio
Creator of Alpine.js
They are the best Google Analytics alternative who
really care
about privacy and simplicity. Fathom’s simple dashboard with clear metrics makes it easy to understand what's happening across all web properties.
Jessica Joyce
SEO Expert
Fathom is a great Google Analytics alternative because it is focused on protecting visitors’ privacy and does not bog down loading times, leading to
faster websites
with
better SEO
.
Nathalie Lussier
Founder of AccessAlly.com
Getting started is simple and easy
Gain valuable insights into your website or web application in just a few steps. And, if you get stuck,
our support team
is always on hand to help you get things up and running.
Step 1
Sign up for an account
Start with a 7-day free trial. Then
pricing starts at $15/month
.
Step 2
Add our script
It’s a single line of code that works with every site, web app and CMS.
Step 3
See your stats
Watch the dashboard fill up in real time.
Our features
How Fathom works
Features you need, no training required
Event tracking
Use custom events to collect user interactions like newsletter sign-ups, link + button clicks or ecommerce sales, and get valuable insights for optimizing your campaigns.
Get detailed reports
Send email reports every week or month so that you can keep track of your metrics without logging in.
Campaigns & newsletters
Use UTMs to collect data on how much traffic your campaigns generate and see how well they convert into revenue.
Keep data forever
That means 20 years from now, customers can still see their data from the day they first started using our software.
Great for your SEO
Our script is lightweight and fast-loading, meaning switching from Google Analytics to Fathom won't hurt your SEO rankings in any way.
Handle any traffic spikes
If an A-list influencer shares your site on social media or your blog post goes viral, your analytics won't slow down or get shut off.
We're happily
Big
Small Tech
We're a small but mighty team and we don't need (or want) funding or investors. The only people we answer to are our customers.
Designed for developers
Our API is easy to use and so powerful we use it ourselves to load data onto customer dashboards in our software.
Integrate with the tools and tech stack you already use
Fathom integrates with everything. Seriously. Our lightweight script is just 2kB in your source code. You can add it to any CMS, framework or technology you're already using. From content management systems like WordPress to progressive frameworks like Next.JS and Vue.JS to other software like ConvertKit, we've got your covered.
Even if you don't see a plugin or integration here, since our embed process is so simple, you can add our analytics script to anything.
See all our of integrations
Trusted by 1,000,000+ websites and counting
Companies worldwide, from startups to the biggest corporations, have switched to Fathom Analytics for its reliability, ease of use and focus on privacy. Our software is a simple and private
Google Analytics alternative
that takes a few minutes to set up and use.
Our new Vue.js website has been using Fathom because I like how it’s
lightweight
and respects user privacy.
Evan You
Creator of Vue.js
Fathom simplifies the task of analyzing site performance while simultaneously respecting my visitors' privacy. Also, it’s a joy to use.
Jon Henshaw
Founder of Coywolf
Today, so many big tech corps are abusing our privacy. This is why I use Fathom. They’re an analytics company
I can trust
.
Justin Jackson
Cofounder of Transistor
I value my time and my website visitors'
right to digital privacy
. That’s why I’ve switched to Fathom.
Marshall Shorts
Founder of Artfluential
Privacy-protections aside, I love the
beautifully simple
interface. Fathom is now part of my default stack.
Derrick Reimer
Founder of SavvyCal
I’m
deeply opposed
to cookie banners. Using Fathom means I don't have to compromise my morals.
Ben Orenstein
Cofounder of Tuple
Ready to simplify your analytics?
Start your free trial, import your GA data, and keep every future visit. Cookie‑free and clutter‑free.
Start a 7-day, unlimited free trial to see how simple analytics can be.
Get started
Check out our full-featured, live demo to see how our software works.
Live demo
We've counted billions of pageviews for thousands of customers, all without ever compromising anyone's digital privacy. We hope you'll
join us
.

  Analytics
After switching to Fathom for my web properties, I find I'm more attentive to my sites because I'm always
        aware of how many people are on them and which pages are most popular.
Caleb Porzio
Creator of Alpine.js
Wow, what a breath of fresh air. I love how simple Fathom Analytics is, and it really gives you all the
        traffic metrics you could need.
Joel Gascoigne
CEO of Buffer
Fathom Analytics is a great Google Analytics alternative because it is focused on protecting visitors’
        privacy and does not bog down loading times, leading to faster websites.
Nathalie Lussier
Founder of AccessAlly.com
Frequently asked questions (and answers)
Will you do a pre-sales call or live demo for us?
We unfortunately don't offer live demos or do calls in our sales process. That said, our software is ridiculously easy to use and you can watch a
10 minute video demo
or click around on
our live demo
. Every
feature is documented
and you can even give our software a 7-day free trial (run it alongside your existing analytics). Of course, if you need help or have specific questions, we're here
via email
—just let us know how we can be of assistance.
Do you have a lower/free option?
Nope, and here’s why: we’ve heavily invested in enterprise-level infrastructure with great speed and reliability (we don’t just host our software on cheap VPS servers that go offline). Our business model is selling software, not your data. So we’ve priced for long-term sustainability of our company. That way we can offer our product for a very long time (forever).
Do you ever do sales or discounts?
Never. Not even for Black Friday. So rest assured, the price you are seeing today or are paying in your account currently is the best price EVER. We’ve never done discounts, nor will we ever. We feel this is fair, because everyone pays the exact same price.
What happens if I hit my pageview limit?
We’ll never turn your analytics off for occasional traffic spikes or if a payment fails the first time (hey, this has happened to all of us at least once). Instead, if you go over for two months in a row, we’ll let you know we’re upgrading you. From there, you can decide to stay or cancel your account (no hard feelings either way).
Can I switch to a different pageview limit?
Of course! If you exceed your pageview limits for two months in a row, we’ll offer to do that for you. Or, if you’re under your current tier for two months in a row (and it’s not our lowest tier already), you can downgrade from within our billing settings.
How many sites does my plan include?
Every plan includes at least 50 sites, and the bigger pageview plans include more (75 on the 1M plan, scaling up to hundreds on the largest plans). You’ll see the exact number for each plan in the
pricing table
. If you need more than your plan includes, you can add sites yourself in packs of 50 for $10/month each (or $100/year, which gets you two months free) from your billing settings. Need to go really big?
Get in touch
and we’ll sort you out.
What if I want to cancel my account or trial?
We’re not interested in having someone’s money if they aren’t happy. So if you want to cancel your account at any point, you can do so right from your settings - you don’t even have to call or email us (unless you have a question). There are no contracts, meaning you can cancel anytime (with a complete data export).
Does custom event tracking count as pageviews?
Yes. If you decide to use our custom event tracking system on your website or use our API, those requests will be counted as if they were pageviews.
What if I have another question?
Reach out
! We will be happy to help with any pre-sales questions.
We've counted billions of pageviews for thousands of customers, all without ever compromising anyone's digital privacy. We hope you'll
join us
.

analysis.name: Fathom Analytics
analysis.tagline: A Google Analytics alternative that's simple & privacy-first
analysis.value_proposition: Privacy-first, cookie-free web analytics that's simple to set up, GDPR-compliant, and shows only real human traffic without slowing down your site.
analysis.ideal_customer: Privacy-conscious website owners, developers, agencies, and content creators who want simple, GDPR-compliant analytics without the complexity or invasiveness of Google Analytics

features.name: cookie-free tracking
features.description: Tracks visitors without using cookies, eliminating the need for cookie consent banners
features.evidence_quote: Fathom anonymizes IP addresses and other visitor data without using cookies

features.name: gdpr compliance
features.description: Fully compliant with GDPR, CCPA, ePrivacy, PECR privacy regulations
features.evidence_quote: fully compliant with GDPR, CCPA, ePrivacy, PECR and more

features.name: ip anonymization
features.description: Anonymizes visitor IP addresses to protect privacy
features.evidence_quote: Fathom anonymizes IP addresses and other visitor data

features.name: single-line script install
features.description: One line of code embeds Fathom on any site, CMS, or framework
features.evidence_quote: Our script is a single line of code that works with any website, CMS or framework

features.name: real-time analytics
features.description: See dashboard fill up in real time as visits happen
features.evidence_quote: start collecting real-time data without prior technical knowledge

features.name: bot detection
features.description: Blocks bots, scrapers, and spam traffic to show only real human visits
features.evidence_quote: Our real-time analytics blocks bots, scrapers and spam traffic—showing you only real, human visits

features.name: advanced bot blocking
features.description: New layer of sophisticated bot protection to keep analytics accurate
features.evidence_quote: New layer of sophisticated bot protection launched to keep your analytics human and accurate

features.name: bot banning
features.description: Additional bot detection layer for cleaner data
features.evidence_quote: A new layer of bot detection to keep your analytics accurate and free from non-human traffic

features.name: event tracking
features.description: Custom events to track sign-ups, clicks, and ecommerce sales
features.evidence_quote: Use custom events to collect user interactions like newsletter sign-ups, link + button clicks or ecommerce sales

features.name: ecommerce tracking
features.description: Track ecommerce conversions and sales as events

features.name: email reports
features.description: Weekly or monthly automated email reports of metrics
features.evidence_quote: Send email reports every week or month so that you can keep track of your metrics without logging in

features.name: utm campaign tracking
features.description: Track UTMs to measure campaign traffic and conversion to revenue
features.evidence_quote: Use UTMs to collect data on how much traffic your campaigns generate

spec.body:
# Zergalytics

**Concept / placeholder.** No repo at `~/zerg/zergalytics/` yet, not in `zstack.yaml`, not on the marketing roadmap as of 2026-05-01.

Captured here because it was named as a future micro-product candidate alongside Zergboard.

## Likely Positioning

Analytics layer for the Zstack family — would unify event/usage data across:

- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zstack/Zergboard|Zergboard]] (board/card/cycle activity)
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zstack/ZergChat|ZergChat]] (channel/thread engagement)
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zstack/ZergCal|ZergCal]] (meeting density, attendance)
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zstack/ZergMeeting|ZergMeeting]] (transcript/action throughput)
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zstack/ZergMail|ZergMail]] / [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zstack/ZMsg|ZMsg]] (comms volume)

Could double as the marketing-side measurement tool currently tracked under MAR/WEB lane prefix `[Measurement]`.

## Open Questions

- Standalone product, or just a dashboard layer surfaced inside Zergboard?
- Internal observability (engineering) vs customer-facing usage analytics — these are usually separate products
- Would compete with PostHog / Mixpanel category if customer-facing

## Next Step

Decide whether Zergalytics is its own microproduct, a feature inside Zergboard, or two distinct things wearing one name. Until decided, keep this as a capture point.

## Related

- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zstack/Zstack|Zstack]] — umbrella

positioning.elevator_pitch: Zergalytics is the AI-native analytics layer for Zstack teams — unified web, product, and cross-app usage intelligence without the surveillance, the breach risk, or the enterprise pricing cliff.
core_differentiator_lines: One analytics layer across every Zstack product you already run.
AI summaries of session replays — no analyst required.
Privacy-compliant by default. No GDPR fine waiting to happen.
Cheaper than Mixpanel before you even open the billing page.
Your board, chat, cal, and comms data in one funnel.

headline_candidates.headline: Analytics that knows your whole stack.
headline_candidates.subhead: Zergalytics unifies event, usage, and behavior data across every Zstack product — no duct tape, no third-party surveillance.

headline_candidates.headline: Stop feeding the beast.
headline_candidates.subhead: Privacy-first analytics built for teams who read the Google Analytics ban headlines and switched anyway.

headline_candidates.headline: Your AI analyst never sleeps.
headline_candidates.subhead: Session replays auto-summarized. Funnels auto-explained. Insights in plain language, not dashboards you'll ignore.

headline_candidates.headline: Mixpanel money. PostHog privacy. Zerg speed.
headline_candidates.subhead: Full-stack analytics — web, product, cross-app — at a price that doesn't require a procurement meeting.

headline_candidates.headline: Analytics without the audit letter.
headline_candidates.subhead: Built GDPR-compliant from day one. No French DPA, no Italian watchdog, no Firefox block.

messaging_pillars.promise: The only analytics product that sees across Zergboard, ZergChat, ZergCal, ZergMeeting, and ZergMail as a single user journey.
proof_points: Connect board activity → meeting throughput → Slack engagement in one funnel — no ETL required.
Cross-product segmentation: see how power Zergboard users behave differently in ZergChat.
Zstack event schema is shared — no custom instrumentation per product.

messaging_pillars.promise: You shouldn't need to hire an analyst to understand your own product.
proof_points: Session replay AI summaries — first in category, per whitespace analysis.
Natural-language funnel explanations: 'Users drop off after the third board creation because X.'
AI-generated insight digests delivered to Fake Matt / ZergChat daily — no dashboard required.

messaging_pillars.promise: GDPR-compliant by default. No surveillance capitalism. No ad-business incentive to harvest your users' data.
proof_points: Google Analytics was declared illegal by French CNIL (2022), Italian Garante (2022), and blocked by Firefox strict mode — Zergalytics is not built on that infrastructure.
Hotjar alternatives gained HN traction specifically because of privacy concerns — we start from that baseline.
No third-party data enrichment or resale model. Your users' data is not our product.

messaging_pillars.promise: You won't replace us with BigQuery and Kubernetes to save money.
proof_points: Mixpanel replacement with BigQuery + K8s is a documented HN cost-escape pattern (191 upvotes) — our pricing doesn't force that conversation.
Zstack bundle pricing: Zergalytics included or heavily discounted for existing Zstack subscribers.
No event volume gotchas. No 'talk to sales' wall at 10M events.

messaging_pillars.promise: No breach headline waiting to happen.
proof_points: Mixpanel's breach exposed OpenAI user data — covered at r/news 1336 upvotes. We treat that as a design constraint, not a post-mortem.
No third-party SDK surface area from ad-tech vendors.
Self-hostable path for security-sensitive teams (roadmap signal from Hotjar/GA community migration patterns).

anti_pitch: We are NOT a surveillance tool monetized by reselling behavioral data — Google Analytics' GDPR bans and Firefox block are what happens when analytics is actually ad infrastructure in disguise.
We are NOT priced to extract maximum value at scale — Mixpanel's cost structure drove teams to rebuild in BigQuery just to escape the bill; we should never be the thing someone replaces.
We are NOT a point solution that ignores the rest of your stack — Plausible and Umami are good web analytics tools but they see nothing beyond the browser; our moat is cross-Zstack context.

We are NOT a privacy-theater product that lists GDPR compliance as a bullet point but still fingerprints users — the community building self-hosted Hotjar alternatives (HN 137 upvotes) chose that path because hosted tools kept failing them.
We are NOT a dashboard dumping ground requiring an analyst to interpret — raw charts are table stakes; AI-synthesized 'here's what actually happened and why' is the product.
positioning.icp_refinement: Primary: founding teams and ops leads (10–200 people) already running or evaluating Zstack products who need cross-product behavioral visibility without standing up a data warehouse. Secondary: privacy-conscious SaaS builders — specifically those who've already migrated away from Google Analytics due to GDPR exposure and are actively searching for a Mixpanel replacement that won't bankrupt them at Series A scale. Anti-ICP: enterprise data teams with dedicated analytics engineers who need raw warehouse access and custom data models — they will outgrow us before we're ready for them.

objection_handling.objection: PostHog is open-source and free — why would we pay for Zergalytics?
objection_handling.response: PostHog is excellent infrastructure for engineering-led teams who want to self-host and maintain it. Zergalytics is for operators who want zero infra overhead and cross-Zstack context PostHog can't see — your Zergboard cycle data and ZergChat engagement don't live in PostHog events unless you build that instrumentation yourself.

objection_handling.objection: Plausible already solves our GA privacy problem and is simpler.
objection_handling.response: Plausible is a best-in-class privacy-first web analytics tool with a deliberate 'no bells and whistles' philosophy. Zergalytics covers web analytics as table stakes but adds product analytics, cross-app funnels, session replay with AI summaries, and Zstack integration — use cases Plausible explicitly doesn't target.

objection_handling.objection: Mixpanel has years of maturity and enterprise integrations we rely on.
objection_handling.response: Mixpanel's maturity also includes a documented security breach exposing OpenAI user data and a cost structure that drives teams to rebuild in BigQuery just to escape the bill. If you're not yet locked into their SDK surface area, the switching cost is lower than the compounding risk of staying.

objection_handling.objection: Google Analytics is free — why pay for anything?
objection_handling.response: Google Analytics is free because your users' behavior is the product funding Google's ad business. The French CNIL, Italian Garante, and Austrian DSB have all declared it illegal under GDPR. Firefox blocks it in strict mode. The cost of 'free' is regulatory exposure and audience blind spots among exactly the tech-savvy users you most want to track.

objection_handling.objection: We're not on Zstack — does Zergalytics work standalone?
objection_handling.response: Yes — web analytics, product event tracking, session replay, and AI insights all work without other Zstack products. The Zstack integration is an amplifier, not a prerequisite. You can adopt Zergalytics today and gain cross-product superpowers if and when you add Zergboard or ZergChat.

differentiation_opportunities.name: Zero-Instrumentation Zstack Event Autocapture
differentiation_opportunities.what: Because all Zstack products share a common SDK and auth layer, Zergalytics auto-captures structured events from Zergboard, ZergChat, ZergCal, ZergMeeting, ZergMail on day 0 — no tracking snippet, no SDK install, no instrumentation work
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: Heap's 'no-code autocapture' still requires per-app installation and JS injection; Mixpanel requires manual event instrumentation; teams churning Mixpanel cite setup cost as reason one
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Requires owning the full productivity stack to share a single event bus. A competitor adding this would need to rebuild or acquire Zergboard/ZergChat/ZergCal — a multi-year effort. PostHog/Heap have no auth-layer or productivity data layer to hook into.
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: HN thread 'How we saved money by replacing Mixpanel' — instrumentation overhead is the second complaint after cost; Heap markets autocapture as differentiator but still requires install

differentiation_opportunities.name: Cross-Product Behavioral Funnels (Web → Chat → Board → Cal)
differentiation_opportunities.what: Build funnels that span web sessions, ZergChat thread engagement, Zergboard card creation, and ZergCal scheduling in a single canvas — e.g., 'user visited /pricing → opened #sales-questions channel → created trial card → booked onboarding call' as one funnel step sequence
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: Every existing analytics tool sees only one layer (web OR product OR CRM); the B2B buyer journey crosses all of them; June/Mixpanel CRM integrations are workarounds via webhook glue that breaks on schema changes
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Requires owning the data primitives for chat, board, calendar, and web simultaneously. Competitors can import CRM events via webhook but cannot natively observe ZergChat thread depth or ZergCal acceptance rates as funnel steps — those data schemas don't exist outside Zstack.
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: June markets 'company-level retention' as differentiated but still requires manual CRM sync; Mixpanel's 'AI that connects across existing data stack' is wrapper language for webhook ingestion, not native cross-product events

differentiation_opportunities.name: Authenticated Cookieless User Journeys via Zstack Auth
differentiation_opportunities.what: Because Zstack products share a single auth layer, Zergalytics stitches authenticated user journeys across marketing pages, product surfaces, and internal tools without cookies, consent banners, or PII storage — the user identity is the Zstack session token, not a third-party cookie
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: Google Analytics ruled illegal by French DPA; Matomo users getting banned from Google Ads; Plausible is cookieless but anonymous-only (no user-level journeys); GDPR enforcement is accelerating across EU
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Requires a shared auth layer that spans marketing site AND product AND comms. GA/Mixpanel/Hotjar have no auth layer — they are always observers, never participants. Plausible is cookieless by design but cannot do user-level stitching at all. Replicating this requires building a full identity platform first.
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: HN 1172 upvotes: GA declared illegal by French DPA; HN 364 upvotes: Matomo users banned from Google Ads — the privacy-compliant segment is growing but no competitor offers user-level journeys without PII

differentiation_opportunities.name: Agent-Triggered A/B Rollout with Zergboard Escalation
differentiation_opportunities.what: An AI agent continuously monitors running experiments, detects statistical significance, and either auto-promotes the winner (for low-stakes tests with pre-authorized thresholds) or creates a Zergboard decision card with the analysis pre-written and posts the result clip to ZergChat — the analyst loop is autonomous end-to-end
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: Every competitor dashboard requires a human to check significance, interpret, and manually push a rollout; agent-as-analyst is a category-defining move as AI shifts from insight to execution
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Requires agents that can read experiment state AND write to a project board AND message a channel in a single transactional context. Mixpanel/Heap have no board or chat surface to write to; wiring this up externally requires webhook chains that break on schema changes and have no rollback.
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: No competitor in the scanned set offers any form of autonomous experiment promotion — all require human dashboard review; Heap/Mixpanel 'AI' is read-only insight generation, never action execution

differentiation_opportunities.name: Session Replay Batch AI Triage → Auto-Generated Zergboard Bug Cards
differentiation_opportunities.what: An agent watches batches of session replays nightly, clusters friction patterns (rage-clicks, dead-ends, error sequences), writes a structured bug/UX finding with screen timestamp + severity, creates a Zergboard card assigned to the relevant owner, and posts the clip URL in ZergChat — zero analyst time required
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: Session replay AI summary is the only listed whitespace in the current scan — no competitor has it; the replay-to-backlog loop is manual at every company using Hotjar/FullStory today
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Requires an agent with simultaneous write access to a replay store, a project board, and a chat system that share identity. Hotjar has no board; Zergboard has no replay; gluing them via Zapier requires custom field mapping that breaks on any schema change. The agent context that makes this coherent only exists inside Zstack.
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: Hotjar privacy complaints and self-hosted alternatives (HN 137 upvotes) show the category is ripe for disruption; zero competitors offer replay → backlog card as a native, autonomous loop

differentiation_opportunities.name: ZergChat Engagement Churn Prediction
differentiation_opportunities.what: Use ZergChat signal (response latency, thread-depth decline, emoji reaction drop, @mention frequency) as leading churn indicators, surfaced as a 'team health score' per account — visible inside Zergalytics and triggering ZergBoard escalation cards when an account goes cold
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: B2B SaaS churn prediction models trained on product events alone miss the 'ghost account' pattern where product logins stay flat but team communication dies first; no competitor has access to internal chat data
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Architecturally impossible without owning the chat layer. June/Mixpanel can import Intercom/Zendesk ticket volume as a proxy but cannot see thread depth, response time distributions, or emoji-reaction decay inside the team's workspace. Requires ZergChat to exist as a native product.
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: June markets 'company-level retention intelligence' as its core differentiator but still relies on product-event proxies for churn signal; no competitor mentions communication-layer signals anywhere in their positioning

differentiation_opportunities.name: Meeting Load vs. Shipping Velocity Correlation Dashboard
differentiation_opportunities.what: Correlate ZergCal meeting density + ZergMeeting action-item completion rate against Zergboard cycle throughput and Zergalytics conversion metrics in a single view — surfaces 'your team's shipping velocity drops 34% in weeks with >12h of meetings'
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: Engineering and product leaders have anecdotal beliefs about meeting overhead but no data; no analytics tool can access both calendar and board data natively; this is a boardroom-ready insight that justifies a subscription on its own
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Requires native access to calendar, meeting transcripts, and a project board simultaneously. Competitors have none of these — they see web/product events only. Linear has board data but no calendar or analytics layer. Notion has calendar but no analytics. Nobody has all three.
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: No competitor in the scanned set offers any team-process ↔ product-outcome correlation; this gap exists because no single vendor owns both the work-management and the measurement layer

differentiation_opportunities.name: Agent-Authored Weekly Metrics Narrative with Zergboard Action Injection
differentiation_opportunities.what: Every Monday, an AI agent reads the prior week's Zergalytics data, writes a plain-English narrative ('conversion dropped 8% — traced to mobile checkout step 3, here are 3 session clips'), creates prioritized Zergboard investigation cards, and posts the summary to a designated ZergChat channel — no human configures this after setup
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: Every analytics tool produces dashboards that require a human to interpret and then manually create follow-up tasks; the insight-to-action gap is where value dies; agent-as-analyst is the category shift happening now
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Requires an agent with read access to metrics AND write access to a board AND chat in one coherent context. Mixpanel's 'AI' generates text only — it cannot create a Zergboard card or post to ZergChat. Wiring this externally requires three separate API auth flows, webhook configs, and breaks when any schema changes.
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: Mixpanel markets 'AI that connects across existing data stack' but it is text generation only, not action execution; Heap/Pendo have no task/board surface to write to

differentiation_opportunities.name: Privacy-Compliant Cohort Analysis Without a Consent Banner
differentiation_opportunities.what: Ship cohort retention analysis (the core Mixpanel/June feature) using only server-side, hashed, non-PII identifiers derived from Zstack auth — users get full cohort funnels with zero GDPR exposure, zero consent banners, zero cookie walls, compliant by architecture not by policy
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: GA ruled illegal in France; Mixpanel had a breach that exposed OpenAI user data; the enterprise compliance burden for using these tools is growing; privacy-native analytics is still a gap — Plausible is cookieless but has no cohort/retention depth
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Requires a server-side identity model tied to a first-party auth layer — competitors built on client-side JS event collection (GA, Mixpanel, Heap, Hotjar) cannot retrofit this without a full rewrite; Plausible could add cohorts but has no user-identity layer by design
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: HN 1214 upvotes: 'Tech-savvy audiences block Google Analytics'; HN 1172 upvotes: GA declared illegal; r/cybersecurity 970 upvotes: Mixpanel breach exposed OpenAI user data — the trust deficit in analytics is real and growing

differentiation_opportunities.name: Zstack-Wide Revenue Attribution Without Ad Spend
differentiation_opportunities.what: Attribute revenue outcomes (Zergboard subscription upgrades, ZergMail campaign responses, ZergChat-assisted deals) to the specific cross-product touchpoints that drove them — without requiring ad pixel data or third-party attribution vendors like Segment or Amplitude
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: Multi-touch attribution requires stitching web, product, email, and CRM events — teams pay Segment $30k+/yr just to pipe data between tools; Zstack collapses this into a single data layer at a fraction of the cost
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Requires owning the full touchpoint surface (web + email + chat + board) under one identity graph. Segment/Rudderstack are middleware that move data between tools that don't share identity; Zergalytics collapses the middleware entirely because the identity is shared at the auth layer.
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: Mixpanel's cost-at-scale complaints (HN 191 upvotes: 'How we saved money by replacing Mixpanel') are partly about the cost of the surrounding data stack (Segment + warehouse + Mixpanel); Zergalytics can eliminate multiple line items at once
